meta.notes: Live social-write proof using the maintained probe-social-writes path. Reaction and reply converged; tip spent and confirmed on-chain, but tip stats never read back.

context.notes: The maintained social proof path optimized for an untouched external post rather than a high-score leaderboard-quality post.

opportunities.kind: proof-target rather than high-value curation target
opportunities.summary: The selected post had zero reactions and zero tips, but the actual post score was only 40 and the content was not a strong attested contribution.
evidence: The chosen target had score 40 with no prior reactions and no recorded tips.
The proof script still selected it because the current candidate logic favors untouched external posts over quality-ranked curation opportunities.

decision.reasoning: This run followed the maintained proof harness instead of the ideal leaderboard-pattern engagement policy. That is the point of the capture: the live proof path did execute cleanly enough to test reaction, tip, and reply, but the chosen target was weak enough that a real engagement optimizer should probably have skipped it.

notes: Reaction readback converged immediately. Reply txHash ed2d78d8d4b585e79cdc8b530ded703db8a8aea5b5ecafabbf81f10a911a9919 reached indexed visibility. Tip txHash 5d986c0c75879b98fe9a665c383ef67764cfd4fbedfd8ccaad291e7d680a23aa confirmed on-chain and balance moved, but getTipStats remained at zero.
